vocab.meaning: người thích làm việc độc lập, cô độc
vocab.example: In primary school, I was a bit of a lone wolf and preferred reading in the library to playing football.

vocab.meaning: trở nên dạn dĩ, cởi mở hơn
vocab.example: I was very introverted initially, but I started to come out of my shell when I joined the school choir.

vocab.meaning: người năng nổ, giao thiệp rộng
vocab.example: Unlike my brother, I wasn't exactly a social butterfly; I found large groups quite overwhelming as a kid.

vocab.meaning: những người có cùng sở thích/tính cách
vocab.example: My best friend and I were two of a kind, so we spent hours playing video games together.

vocab.meaning: tách biệt bản thân khỏi mọi người
vocab.example: I didn't want to cut myself off from my peers, but I definitely needed solitary time to recharge.

vocab.meaning: một người để dựa dẫm, chia sẻ
vocab.example: Even as a child, I valued having a close friend as a shoulder to lean on during difficult school days.

vocab.meaning: sống khép kín, ít giao tiếp
vocab.example: I tended to keep to myself when I was young because I enjoyed imaginative solo play.

vocab.word: birds of a feather flock together
vocab.meaning: ngưu tầm ngưu, mã tầm mã
vocab.example: We were all bookworms, and as they say, birds of a feather flock together, so we always hung out in the library.

vocab.word: get on like a house on fire
vocab.ipa: /ɡet ɒn laɪk ə haʊs ɒn ˈfaɪə/
vocab.meaning: thân thiết với nhau rất nhanh
vocab.example: Whenever my cousins visited, we got on like a house on fire and played outside until sunset.

vocab.meaning: mở đường cho, tạo điều kiện cho
vocab.example: Collaborating on group projects in primary school paved the way for my current teamwork skills.
